license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.nyxdfxn
title: Salty sample derivatization protocol for GC-MS
author: Emilia Sogin

steps_raw.instruction: Dry 0.5 to 1 ml of a salty sample for 6 hr using an eppendorf Concentrator plus SpeedVac at 45 °C in V-AQ mode. Critical step: the drying in the SpeedVac should take no longer than 6h, otherwise the samples could degrade. Samples can be kept in SpeedVac overnight after the run
After removal from the SpeedVac, resuspend the sample in 250 µL of anhydrous toluene
Incubate the sample at 37 °C for 30 min and 1350 r.p.m. using a thermomixer

Transfer 100 µL of the clear supernatan into a GC-MS vial insert and place on autosample for GC-MS analysis Critical step: the samples should be run within 1 day after the preparation, otherwise they will undergo degradation and the MeOX will crystalize, making the injection impossible
Ultrasonicate the derivatized sample for 10 min
Ultrasonicat the sample for 10 min at 100% effiency in a sonication bath

Remove solvant under a stead flow of N2 gas. Improtant note: Prior to inserting needles into tubes, make sure they pre-cleaned with organic solvent. Regulate gas flow so only the surface of the solvent is disturbed. Needles should not touch the solvent. a 3-D printed drying device with attached needles.
Samples can be stored at 4°C until derivatization.
Vortex the sample to dissolve salt crystals into the pyridine solvent
Add 80 µL of Methoxyamine (MeOx; 20 mg/1 mL of pyridine) to dried sample. a The salt crystals should dissolve into the pyridine creating a milky white solution as pictured.
Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an room temperature bath
Incubate the sample at 37°C for 90 minutes and 1350 r.p.m. using a thermomixer
Remove pyridine solvent from sample leaving behind salt crystals under a stead flow of N2 gas. Use a thermomixer to maintain constant temperature (25°C) and rotational speed (200 r.p.m.).
Once sample is dry, add 100 μL of N,O-Bis(trimethylsilyl)trifluoroacetamide (BSTFA) to salt pellet. Note: Do not leave tubes open for extended periods of time to avoid added moisture from the air
Ultrasonicate for 10 min using an ultrasonication bath at room temperature
Pellet salt by centrifuging for 2 min at 14,800 rpm
